heading: Implementation in acquisition of construction services and materials for Federal facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Construction services and materials for Federal facilities shall be procured in accordance with the policies and procedures set forth in the provisions of title 10, referred to in section 3016 of such title as “chapter 137 legacy provisions”, section 3453 of such title, division C (except sections 3302, 3307(e), 3501(b), 3509, 3906, 4710, and 4711) of subtitle I of title 41, and . Determination of a design method shall be based upon preliminary market research as required under and . If the requirements of this subchapter conflict with the provisions of or section 3307(b) to (d) of title 41, then the provisions of such sections 3453 or 3307(b) to (d) shall take precedence.section 205b(2) of this titlesection 3453(c) of title 10section 3307(d) of title 41section 3453 of title 101So in original. Probably should be “section”.1

hard-metric specifications are necessary in a contract for the repair or replacement of parts of Federal facilities in existence or under construction upon the effective date of the Savings in Construction Act of 1996; or

children.label: A
children.text: the application requires hard-metric concrete masonry units to coordinate dimensionally into 100 millimeter building modules; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total installed price of hard-metric concrete masonry units is estimated to be equal to or less than the total installed price of using non-hard-metric concrete masonry units. Total installed price estimates shall be based, to the extent available, on cost or pricing data or price analysis, using actual hard-metric and non-hard-metric offers received for comparable existing projects. The head of the agency shall include in the writing required in this subsection an explanation of the factors used to develop the price estimates.
the predominant voluntary industry consensus standards include the use of hard-metric for the items specified; or

hard-metric specifications are necessary in a contract for the repair or replacement of parts of Federal facilities in existence or under construction upon the effective date of the Savings in Construction Act of 1996; or

children.label: A
children.text: the application requires hard-metric recessed lighting fixtures to coordinate dimensionally into 100 millimeter building modules; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total installed price of hard-metric recessed lighting fixtures is estimated to be equal to or less than the total installed price of using non-hard-metric recessed lighting fixtures. Total installed price estimates shall be based, to the extent available, on cost or pricing data or price analysis, using actual hard-metric and non-hard-metric offers received for comparable existing projects. The head of the agency shall include in the writing required in this subsection an explanation of the factors used to develop the price estimates.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: d Limitation
children.text: The provisions of subsections (b) and (c) of this section shall not apply to Federal contracts to acquire construction products for the construction of facilities outside of the United States and its territories.

children.label: A
children.text: guidance or regulations issued by the agency on the use of the metric system of measurement in contracts for the construction of Federal buildings; and

children.label: B
children.text: the use of the metric system of measurement for services and materials required for incorporation in individual projects to construct Federal buildings.

The ombudsman shall be responsible for ensuring that the agency is not implementing the metric system of measurement in a manner that is impractical or is likely to cause significant inefficiencies or loss of markets to United States firms in violation of the policy stated in , or is otherwise inconsistent with guidance issued by the Secretary of Commerce in consultation with the Interagency Council on Metric Policy while ensuring that the goals of this subchapter are observed.section 205b(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: whether the agency is adequately applying the policies and procedures in this section;

children.label: B
children.text: whether the availability of hard-metric products and services from United States firms is sufficient to ensure full and open competition; and

children.label: C
children.text: the total installed price to the Federal Government.

After the head of the agency has rendered a decision regarding a recommendation of the ombudsman, the ombudsman shall be responsible for communicating the decision to all appropriate policy, design, planning, procurement, and notifying personnel in the agency. The ombudsman shall conduct appropriate monitoring as required to ensure the decision is implemented, and may submit further recommendations, as needed. The head of the agency’s decision on the ombudsman’s recommendations, and any supporting documentation, shall be provided to affected parties and made available to the public in a timely manner.
Nothing in this section shall be construed to supersede the bid protest process established under subchapter V of chapter 35 of title 31.
